topic: Mamdani NYC mayor race - key facts
summary: Mamdani is the Democratic nominee, leads polls by 18-20 points in 4-way race, election Nov 4 2025
content:
Key facts from Wikipedia (updated Sept 15, 2025):
- Mamdani won Democratic primary June 24, defeating Cuomo 56.4% to 43.6% (RCV final round)
- General election: Nov 4, 2025 (FPTP)
- 4-way race: Mamdani (D), Cuomo (Fight and Deliver/Independent), Sliwa (R), Adams (Independent)
- Poll averages (Sept 9): Mamdani 43%, Cuomo 25%, Sliwa 14%, Adams 10%
- Mamdani +18.5 in poll averages
- Opposition has failed to consolidate - Adams refused to drop out Sept 5, Sliwa staying in, Cuomo running independently
- Walden dropped out Sept 2 and urged consolidation, but no one followed
- NYC is overwhelmingly Democratic (~80% registration)
- Recent NYC general elections: Democratic nominee wins with 65-75% typically
- Last non-Democrat to win: Bloomberg (2001-2009) in very unusual circumstances
